chapters.title: At the end of a path, the next one begins
chapters.text:
After the end of a fantastic soccer career in 2015, Abby Wambach chose to leave her biggest support, her soccer team, and now she was alone in her attempts to cope with the challenges on her life path. But she needed to support herself, fight injustice, and advocate for equal rights — women league players earn significantly less than men.

Even after the years of winning prizes and all possible victories, the athlete concluded that she was the only reason for her limitations. She decided to accept all the challenges and deal with them by herself. She set her priorities and began to act and inspire others. Thanks to her constant work on herself, she felt her strength, heard her inner voice, and stopped being afraid of her ambitions.

The strength with which you want to fulfill your desires directly affects how you manage to expand the boundaries of your capabilities.

On this new path, she met many people, including a large number of women. Talking to them showed her that women are limited in self-realization and the opportunities to express themselves in sports, other professional areas, and even in everyday life. Decade by decade, they were taught to be silent, agree with everything, and be satisfied with the little they got.

She was shocked that such a position in society remains the only possible one for many women in the 21st century. She saw the solution to this problem in creating leadership programs for women. Regular meetings and workshops would allow them to feel the support of the people around them and become more confident in developing their careers. Wambach embodied this idea by becoming a co-founder of the Wolfpack Endeavor project.

Progress is impossible where time has stopped and minds are closed to changes.

The author of the book Wolfpack has learned to be a Wolf and wants to help others. She invites her readers to get on their four paws with confidence and go in search of their pack. In this summary, you will find the fundamental laws of Wambach’s life, which will allow you to develop enough courage to create anything, love anyone, and be the person you have long dreamed of becoming.

Did you know? There is a Latin expression Homo homini lupus est means A man is a wolf to another man. And our ancestors used it to describe selfish people and their mean actions towards others.

chapters.title: A wolf looks at you from the mirror every day
chapters.text:
It's time to deal with the wolves.
What do they have to do with this book? To understand this, we need to call to mind one story.

People didn't see wolves in Yellowstone for many years until 1995. It seemed that they would damage the well-established work of the ecosystem that existed without them. In reality, they became lifesavers; it turned out that the wolves did not damage the ecosystem but rather saved and improved its processes.

Thanks to the wolves, the number of deer decreased, allowing the restoration of the green space eaten away by herbivores. Trees and bushes strengthened the riverbank and attracted other animals to feed on their leaves and berries. For Abby Wambach, such wolves in human society are people who are not afraid to take new paths to achieve their goals. They boldly abandon the old rules of the game, invent new ones, and support others in their attempts. At the same time, the wolves always understand the value of what they have. It inspires them and does not reduce their desire to reach the next peak.

You don't have to compare yourself to others to know you deserve more. Create your ideal picture of life and start taking steps towards its fulfillment.

Each time people tell you what to do, making you doubt your choices and limit your actions, they are trying to stop you from becoming a Wolf. In such situations, it makes no sense to wait for help from someone else. Catch the wolf's eye in your mirror and start saving yourself.

chapters.title: Sometimes a leader has to step aside
chapters.text:
People in our society need to train themselves to look for manifestations of leadership not in others but in themselves. It is not necessary to be a space explorer, the president of a large company, or an Olympic champion (although Abby Wambach decided to do the latter). After all, the fact is that leadership does not always require being in the forefront and undertaking permanent daily actions.

The old leadership rules have tried to instill this habit in everyone: in no case should you leave the game; each stop increases the gap between you and your partners who will want to take your place. But we are fortunate because the reality is quite different.

Looking back at her past, Abby Wambach realizes that every Wolf she met was a leader. But her approach is different from the one mentioned earlier. For her, as a team player, being a leader means being ready to transfer responsibility to someone else at the right moment. In such situations, leadership qualities allowed her not to interfere with others and observe from the sidelines.

Wambach had to make this decision during the final World Cup. She remembered this game because she watched it from the bench. Being an ambitious team captain, she decided in the first second of the game that the brightest day of her life would turn into a complete failure. However, her team needed her support as a fan at that moment. She reacted to this request with lightning speed, shouting encouraging words to the other players, bringing them water during the breaks, and rejoicing at their well-deserved victory. That day, she took care of the team's rear and proved to herself, the players, and her fans that she remains a leader under any circumstances.

If you’re not a leader on the bench, don’t call yourself a leader on the field. You’re either a leader everywhere or nowhere. ~ Abby Wambach

The first time you have to give up your place to someone else and watch the process from the side, you will feel doubt. It will seem that you will lose everything that you have achieved. But if you pass this test, you will be very grateful for the valuable lesson.

As real leaders, every Wolf knows how to loudly declare themselves, be it standing on a stage in front of a large number of people or sitting in the last row.

chapters.title: Fall seven, rise eight
chapters.text:
One of the most vivid memories of the young athlete Abby Wambach was visiting the U.S. Women’s National Team’s locker room. She was shocked: the last thing these players saw before entering the field was a photograph of the Norwegian national team celebrating their victory over the United States. Strange motivation, right?

Sometimes you may have situations when people let you down or leave you with empty promises. It can also be a feeling that you don’t have time for something, can’t cope, or are doing the wrong things. At times like these comes suffering; you can be angry and upset. You suddenly find yourself vulnerable, and people around you notice this. It is normal to be torn with anger and have a sense of injustice in this case. But it’s abnormal and impossible to give up and stay put.

But failure is an opportunity to see other options for solving a problem. Or on the contrary, it is a chance to recognize that the time has come to change direction radically and try something else. In any case, you should not leave your plans and desires; just change the route.

When it's hard for you to make a decision, try to change your perspective from “what if” to “even if” and continue on your way.

Wolves are not afraid to take risks repeatedly to get what they want. And they are by no means ashamed of failing.

Let a loss strengthen you and charge you for your next victory. Failure is essential information for the future. Each attempt brings you closer to the moment when everything works out.

chapters.title: There are enough opportunities to shine for everyone, so share them with others
chapters.text:
Our society loves to celebrate individual victories; the whole world reads the biographies of these people to understand how they managed to achieve such success. Everyone wants to know everything — what organizations they were in as children, who taught them at school, with whom they wrote their thesis, who was with them during their first failures, who inspired them. When will people finally admit that so-called “individual” achievements have collective status?

Previous generations have worked hard to make it possible for you to be where you are today. Try to thank publicly those who made your success possible. The realization that so many people are behind you can give you a huge boost of confidence! There will be more inspiring stories when we all start to celebrate each other's victories together. After all, every achievement is our common step towards solving a problem and improving our world.

It might appear to the crowd that the team is celebrating the goal scorer, but what the team is really celebrating is every player, every coach, every practice, every sprint, every doubt, and every failure that this one single goal represents. ~ Abby Wambach

At the same time, it is vital not to compare your progress with others. But if you think in terms of lack, you reduce all your interests to empty rivalry. The earth's resources are finite, and fortunately, this does not apply to opportunities for people. Your partner's success hasn't made it impossible for you to succeed. Don't think of the world as a pie with a limited number of slices. The world is more like the sun; the light and warmth are enough for everyone.

Find a like-minded person with whom you will regularly share the steps you take to achieve your goals. Over time, evaluate how such team reporting affects your motivation and productivity.

Wolves surround themselves with those who help them become the best versions of themselves.

Be thankful and talk about people who ignited a spark in you with their actions, celebrate their victories, and then try to inspire others.

chapters.title: Say it loud
chapters.text:
Every victory allows you to improve yourself and your skills. So, each time you win, try to cover more and more people with your influence. Teach them to be your followers and leaders who can take others under their wings.

Your honest stories about your good and bad decisions will help others see you as an actual human. When you share knowledge and open up to your team, you teach them to be bold and curious.

No one knows your strengths better than you. So, never be afraid to declare loudly what you know, deserve, and aspire to; humility and the game of modesty have not done anyone any good for a long time. It's time to be the principal advocate and speaker of your ideas.

If you are unsure about your public speaking skills, think about the five people you communicate with most often. How do they talk about their field of work and manage to defend their interests? You can learn from them.

Wolves don't try to diminish the attention they get and the space they occupy.

Share honest stories with your like-minded people. Demand improvements and opportunities to prove yourself and your pack's skills. Such behavior will serve as an excellent example for others. With each person, the common voice will sound louder and more confident.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
The wolf's image and its meaning have long attracted researchers and writers. Different cultures attach different connotations; while some expect danger and unpredictability, others see wolves as carriers of strength, courage, and even loyalty.

Athlete and activist Abby Wambach presents this image in her unique way. She invites us all to transform into wolves and be the first ones to help ourselves in critical situations. She developed this approach as a strategy for encouraging and supporting women within her program, Wolfpack Endeavor. But over time, it became clear that this idea allows everyone to take a fresh look at their lives.

Wambach uses examples from her life to prove that every event offers a valuable experience. And this is how you can gain essential knowledge, too, regardless of whether you succeeded in achieving your goal or if everything turned out to be a fiasco. You will not be left alone with your problems because you will have your pack.

The decision to become a Wolf is made quickly and irrevocably, meaning you will no longer allow anyone to control your life. You will make decisions for yourself, recharge from your victories and failures, and take care of your pack.

Freedom, unlimited opportunities, and a chance to meet your future Self are already waiting for you.

Are you ready to become a Wolf?

We are the Wolves. There is magic inside of us. There is power among us. Let’s unleash and unite. Let’s storm these valleys together and change the game forever. ~ Abby Wambach

Try this

• What do you dream of getting, feeling, and experiencing? Imagine with whom you are ready to share these new sensations, then surround yourself with the right people and form your pack.
• Write two letters, one to yourself in the past and one to yourself in the future. In the first one, praise yourself for what you have been able to achieve during this time. In the second letter, create an image of who you want to be and plan how you can achieve it.
• In which areas have you never worked before, but your skills would be helpful there? Take a risk and try to find a job there. It may open a door that you would never think to knock on.

Read it till the end?
